get.description: Return 200 only when both Postgres and Redis are reachable, else 503.

Compose and store a single-use SIWE challenge for ``address``.

The full EIP-4361 message is built here, from server settings, and persisted. The
wallet signs it verbatim; /auth/verify checks against the stored copy. That is why
``domain`` and ``chainId`` cannot be forged: the client never states them.

parameters.name: address

schema.description: The wallet address that will sign.
schema.title: Address

parameters.description: The wallet address that will sign.

post.description:
Verify a signed challenge and return a session credential.

Order matters: the nonce is claimed (atomically, once) before the signature is
checked, so a replay cannot ride on a slow verification.
Mint a long-lived key and return its plaintext — the only time it is ever shown.

get.description: List this developer's programmatic keys. No plaintext — it is not recoverable.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Developers Me Keys Get

delete.description: Revoke a key. It stops authenticating on the next request that presents it.

get.description:
Catalogue models, each flagged with whether a node is serving it right now.

Availability is reported rather than filtered: a developer whose model went dark
should see that it exists and is offline, not watch it vanish from the list.

post.description:
Run a chat completion on the network and bill the tokens it used.

With `stream=true` the reply is an SSE stream of OpenAI `chat.completion.chunk` events.
The billing invariants do not change with the shape: the worst case is still reserved
before a node is touched, and the hold is still resolved exactly once — see
`app.streaming_chat` for what "exactly once" has to survive when the client hangs up
mid-generation.

200.description: A completed chat completion. With `stream=true` the response is instead an OpenAI-compatible SSE stream of `chat.completion.chunk` events terminated by `data: [DONE]`, with usage and `cost_usdc` on the final event.

501.description: data_tier=confidential_tee is not supported on the chat path.

post.description: Generate images on the network and bill per image returned.

get.description: What the free tier serves. Deliberately not the paid catalogue.

schema.title: Response Public Models Public Models Get

post.description:
Stream a free chat completion. No account, no balance, no ledger entry.

Streamed rather than unary because it is what makes the queue tolerable: a caller who
waited for a slot starts seeing tokens the moment they get one.

get.description:
How much of today's image allowance this WALLET has left.

Gated on the same wallet session as generation itself: the allowance belongs to an
address, so there is no answer to give a caller who has not proved they hold one. It
also means the number the UI shows is the number that will actually be enforced, rather
than a guess made against a different anchor.

schema.title: Response Image Quota Public Images Quota Get

post.description:
Generate an image. Requires a WALLET SESSION — unlike chat, which stays anonymous.

The asymmetry is the point. Chat is cheap to serve and cheap to get wrong, so it is
open. Image generation is neither: it is the surface where a prompt filter has to hold,
and a filter is worth far more when the request belongs to an identity than when it
comes from an address behind a NAT. Requiring a session is what makes the quota
countable, the refusals attributable, and repeat abuse something that can be acted on.

Order of checks, each refusing before the next costs anything:
  1. a wallet session at all (the dependency),
  2. prompt screening — before a node, before the quota is spent,
  3. the daily allowance for this wallet.

Screening runs BEFORE the quota so a refused prompt does not consume an allowance. A
caller whose prompt is rejected has not had an image, and charging them for one would
turn the filter into a way to burn someone else's day.

schema.title: Response Public Image Public Images Post

post.description: Create a developer and return its API key exactly once.

get.description:
Every ledger leg this developer's money moved through, newest first.

Ordered by group so the two (or more) legs of a transaction stay adjacent.

Both kinds of movement, and it used to be only one: an inner join on ``job_id`` meant
a row had to belong to a job to appear, so on-chain deposits — which have no job —
were invisible. A developer could top up 50 USDC, watch their balance rise, and find
nothing in their statement to explain it.

A group is theirs if any leg names them, which is what pulls in whole deposit groups
(the protocol leg carries no ``account_ref``), or if it came from one of their jobs.
Every leg of a matching group is returned, so the UI can still show that each
transaction balances.

schema.title: Response My Ledger Billing Ledger Get

get.description: Authoritative period totals derived from the developer's ledger.

post.description: Store an input blob and return its ref for use as a job's ``input_ref``.
Begin a resumable upload.

get.description: Return the current offset (bytes received) so the client can resume.

patch.description: Append a chunk at ``Upload-Offset``. A mismatched offset is a 409 (retry after GET).

post.description: Assemble the staged chunks, verify the declared digest, and promote to a blob.

Validate, escrow funds for, and enqueue a job.

The job lands in ``queued`` and its id goes on the queue; the scheduler (Session 3)
matches it to a provider. Worst-case cost is escrowed now and reconciled at
settlement. Re-submitting with the same ``Idempotency-Key`` returns the original job
instead of creating a duplicate.

get.description: List the caller's own jobs, newest first.
Read one of the caller's own jobs.
Stream the result blob of a completed job the caller owns.
Return the full audit trail for a job: attempts and ledger movements.

Stream job-status changes for the authenticated developer as SSE.

The client establishes its baseline with a normal `GET /jobs` and then
connects here for deltas; on reconnect it refetches to close any gap.

post.description:
Add the provider capability to the signed-in address, and mint its node's agent key.

Gated on a wallet session rather than merely on developer credentials: this mints a
credential, and a key that can mint keys makes revocation meaningless (see
``require_wallet_session``). A leaked API key must not be able to stand up a node.

The returned key is for the NODE — a machine that reads it from its environment. The
operator never signs into the console with it; they sign in with the same wallet they
used here.

get.description: Return the authenticated provider's current record.

patch.description: Partially update declared capabilities. Only provided fields change.

get.description: Return this provider's byte counters — lifetime and for the current session.
Return the provider's latest benchmark report, or null if none submitted.

schema.title: Response My Benchmark Providers Me Benchmark Get

get.description: Return this provider's execution history — one row per attempt, newest first.

schema.title: Response My Jobs Providers Me Jobs Get

get.description: Return this provider's reputation events — why the score moved, newest first.

schema.title: Response My Reputation Providers Me Reputation Get

get.description: Report the provider's trust source (Session 11.3): attested / benchmark / self_report.

schema.title: Response My Trust Providers Me Trust Get

post.description:
Long-poll for work: return immediately if a job is assigned, else hold the
connection open up to ``poll_hold_seconds`` before returning empty.

Holding the request open keeps the control channel warm across idle periods while
still surfacing new work within a poll tick of it being assigned.
Idle keepalive: refresh presence so the coordinator knows the agent is alive.

Negotiate the reachability path from the agent's NAT report.

A direct P2P path is chosen when the NAT topology allows it (open / restricted cone);
a symmetric NAT can't be punched, so the session uses the relay. The choice is
recorded on the provider and logged. Actual hole punching happens out of band; a
direct send that fails still falls back to relay transparently (ProviderChannel).

Sync the provider's cached artifact digests (Session 8.5 locality hint).

Replaces the recorded set so it tracks the agent's LRU cache; the scheduler then
soft-prefers this provider for jobs whose input it already holds.

get.description:
Return where to fetch an artifact from (Session 8.7). Origin unless peer
distribution is enabled and another provider already seeds the digest.

post.description: Extend the lease on an in-flight job so the reaper does not reclaim it.

Agent reports it has begun executing: ``assigned → running``.

For a redundant job the first provider to report moves the job to ``running``; the
others find it already there and only advance their own attempt — the per-attempt state
is what matters, the job status is shared.

Store a signed onboarding benchmark and validate it against declared hardware.

The report is attributed to the authenticated provider; validation (Session 11.2)
catches a machine claiming hardware it can't benchmark to.

Ingest a telemetry sample and flag the provider degraded if a signal crosses a
threshold (Session 11.4). Recovery telemetry clears the flag.

Verify a TEE attestation quote and set the provider's attested flag (Session 9.5).

A valid quote grants ``tee_attested`` (enabling confidential-tee assignment and key
release); a tampered or absent quote clears it and is rejected (400).

get.description:
Release the job's data key to its assigned agent (Session 9.3).

Only the provider the job is assigned to receives it, and only while the job is in
flight — the key is job-scoped and expires with the job.

Mint short-lived, job-scoped secrets for the assigned agent (Session 9.6).

Values are never persisted and never logged (only their names); they expire on their
own and are unavailable once the job ends.
Stream the input blob for a job assigned to this provider.

post.description: Store a result blob and return its (content-addressed) ref for the result call.

Accept a result + proof and move the job to its terminal state.

The result is verified (proof, exit/timeout, canary match) and, once enough attempts
are in, the job finalizes by quorum — settling the winner and slashing cheats.

get.description: List the calling provider's disputes, newest first.

schema.title: Response My Disputes Disputes Me Get

get.description:
Operator view: disputes awaiting manual adjudication, oldest first (10.4).

Declared before ``/disputes/{dispute_id}`` so the literal path isn't captured as an id.

schema.title: Response Review Queue Disputes Review Queue Get

get.description: Return one of the provider's disputes with the full evidence that triggered it.

post.description: Contest an open slash within its window → moves it to review.
Operator records a ruling on an under-review dispute, with an audit-logged reason.

get.description: Return the routed URL + capability token for an endpoint job the caller owns.

Expose current metrics in Prometheus text format.

Gated behind the operator secret (M7): the scrape leaks ledger totals, provider counts,
and queue depth, so it is not world-readable. Prometheus scrapes it with the operator
bearer; an unauthenticated request gets 401.

Ack.title: Ack
Ack.description: Generic acknowledgement with the resulting job status.

AgentJob.title: AgentJob
AgentJob.description: Everything an agent needs to run one assigned job.

AgentPathReport.title: AgentPathReport
AgentPathReport.description:
Agent reports its NAT classification + candidates so the coordinator can decide
whether a direct path is possible or the relay must be used.

AgentPollResponse.title: AgentPollResponse
AgentPollResponse.description:
Poll result — a job to run, or ``job=null`` when the queue for this provider is
empty.

AgentStatusRequest.title: AgentStatusRequest
AgentStatusRequest.description: Agent reports it has started executing (``running``).

ApiKeyResponse.title: ApiKeyResponse
ApiKeyResponse.description:
A programmatic key as listed in /settings. The secret is NOT included — it is
returned once, by the create call, and never again.

AttemptRecord.title: AttemptRecord
AttemptRecord.description: One execution attempt in a job's audit trail.

AttestationQuote.title: AttestationQuote
AttestationQuote.description: A TEE attestation quote submitted by the agent (Session 9.5).

AttestationResult.title: AttestationResult
AttestationResult.description: Whether the submitted attestation was accepted.

BandwidthResponse.title: BandwidthResponse
BandwidthResponse.description: Per-provider byte counters, lifetime and for the current session (Session 7.7).

BenchmarkSubmit.title: BenchmarkSubmit
BenchmarkSubmit.description: A signed benchmark report submitted at onboarding (Session 11.1).

BillingLedgerEntry.title: BillingLedgerEntry
BillingLedgerEntry.description:
One double-entry ledger row across the developer's jobs (Session 10.1).

Every leg is returned raw — the frontend groups by ``entry_group`` to show that
each transaction balances (sum of debits == sum of credits). Numbers are never
"tidied" client-side; a mismatch is a bug to surface, not to hide.

BillingSummary.title: BillingSummary
BillingSummary.description:
Authoritative period totals derived from the ledger (Session 10.3).

``total_spent == provider_paid + protocol_fees + data_costs``. All values are
computed on the backend so the UI shows exact, auditable figures.

BlobRef.title: BlobRef
BlobRef.description: A stored blob's content-addressed ref and size.

CacheReport.title: CacheReport
CacheReport.description: Agent reports the digests it currently has cached (Session 8.5).

ChatChoice.title: ChatChoice
ChatChoice.description:
One completion. The network returns exactly one: ``n`` is not offered on the
request, so there is never a second choice to report.

ChatCompletionMessage.title: ChatCompletionMessage
ChatCompletionMessage.description: The assistant's reply, in the envelope an OpenAI client expects to unwrap.

ChatCompletionRequest.title: ChatCompletionRequest
ChatCompletionRequest.description:
A chat request. Bounded at the edge: these numbers size the balance gate, and an
unbounded max_tokens would make the worst case unknowable.

created.title: Created
created.description: Unix seconds, as OpenAI reports it.

ChatCompletionResponse.title: ChatCompletionResponse
ChatCompletionResponse.description:
A completed chat request, shaped as the OpenAI API shapes it.

This sits at an OpenAI path and accepts an OpenAI request, so it has to answer with an
OpenAI response. The previous shape (``{model, content, usage, ...}``) was close enough
to invite an SDK and wrong enough to break it on the first read of ``choices`` — and
that break lands *after* a developer has integrated, which is worse than an interface
that is plainly different from the start.

``cost_usdc`` and ``provider_id`` stay as top-level EXTRAS, the pattern OpenRouter uses:
an OpenAI client ignores fields it does not know, so nothing breaks, and the two
Gridix-specific facts a developer actually wants — what this cost, who served it — stay
one field away instead of being discarded for the sake of conformance.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: One turn. OpenAI-shaped so existing clients work unchanged.

ChatUsage.title: ChatUsage
ChatUsage.description:
Tokens the request actually consumed — what it is billed on.

``total_tokens`` is a computed field rather than a plain property because OpenAI carries
it on the wire, and a property is invisible to serialisation: a client reading
``usage.total_tokens`` would find nothing there.

CreateApiKeyRequest.title: CreateApiKeyRequest
CreateApiKeyRequest.description: Mint a programmatic key for the agent/API.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: Store this now — it is never shown again.

CreatedApiKey.title: CreatedApiKey
CreatedApiKey.description: The one and only time a programmatic key's plaintext is returned.

DataKeyResponse.title: DataKeyResponse
DataKeyResponse.description: The brokered per-job data key released to the assigned agent (Session 9.3).

DataTier.title: DataTier
DataTier.description: Per-job data-handling policy (Session 9). Guarantees rise with the tier.

DisputeResponse.title: DisputeResponse
DisputeResponse.description: A slash dispute with its reproducible evidence.

DisputeRuling.title: DisputeRuling
DisputeRuling.description: An operator's manual decision on a dispute (Session 10.4).

EndpointInfo.title: EndpointInfo
EndpointInfo.description: The coordinator-issued routed URL + capability token for an endpoint job.

GeneratedImage.title: GeneratedImage
GeneratedImage.description:
One generated image.

Only ``url`` is populated: nodes return a reference to the stored artefact, never
inline bytes. ``b64_json`` — the other half of OpenAI's image object — is deliberately
absent rather than present-and-always-null, because a field that is always null is a
promise the network does not keep.

HealthResult.title: HealthResult
HealthResult.description: The evaluated health verdict for a reported sample.

HeartbeatRequest.title: HeartbeatRequest
HeartbeatRequest.description: Extend the lease on an in-flight job.

IceCandidate.title: IceCandidate
IceCandidate.description: A single ICE-style connectivity candidate advertised by the agent.

created.title: Created
created.description: Unix seconds, as OpenAI reports it.

ImageGenerationResponse.title: ImageGenerationResponse
ImageGenerationResponse.description:
Generated images, shaped as the OpenAI images API shapes them.

Same reasoning as ChatCompletionResponse: OpenAI path, OpenAI request, so an OpenAI
envelope — ``{created, data:[...]}`` — with the Gridix facts kept as extras.

JobArgs.title: JobArgs
JobArgs.description: Optional container overrides for the job.

JobAudit.title: JobAudit
JobAudit.description: The full audit trail for one job: lifecycle, attempts, and money movements.

JobKind.title: JobKind
JobKind.description: Job classification. Canaries are indistinguishable from real work to providers.

JobResponse.title: JobResponse
JobResponse.description: A job as seen by its developer.

JobSecretsResponse.title: JobSecretsResponse
JobSecretsResponse.description: Short-lived, job-scoped secrets injected into the container (Session 9.6).

JobStatus.title: JobStatus
JobStatus.description: States in the job lifecycle. Transitions are enforced by ``app.state_machine``.

LedgerRecord.title: LedgerRecord
LedgerRecord.description: One ledger row in a job's audit trail.

ModelInfo.title: ModelInfo
ModelInfo.description: A catalogue model and whether the network is serving it right now.

ModelsResponse.title: ModelsResponse
ModelsResponse.description: Everything GRIDIX can serve, with prices.

NonceResponse.title: NonceResponse
NonceResponse.description:
A challenge to sign. ``message`` is composed server-side and must be signed
byte-for-byte — the client never assembles the text it authenticates with.

PathResponse.title: PathResponse
PathResponse.description: The negotiated path for the provider's current session.

PeerFetchPlan.title: PeerFetchPlan
PeerFetchPlan.description: Where a provider should fetch an artifact from (Session 8.7).

PingResponse.title: PingResponse
PingResponse.description: Presence status returned to an agent keepalive (Session 7.1).

ProviderCapabilities.title: ProviderCapabilities
ProviderCapabilities.description: Capabilities a provider declares. All fields optional on PATCH (partial update).

ProviderJobAttempt.title: ProviderJobAttempt
ProviderJobAttempt.description:
One execution this provider ran, for the provider job-history view (Session 11.6).

Joins the attempt to its parent job so a provider sees what it worked on, how it
turned out, and how long it took — without exposing another developer's job payload.

ProviderResponse.title: ProviderResponse
ProviderResponse.description: A provider's own view of itself.

PublicChatRequest.title: PublicChatRequest
PublicChatRequest.description:
A free chat turn.

``model`` is accepted but not trusted: whatever arrives, the served model is the free
one. Taking the caller's value would make the allowlist a suggestion.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: Store this now — it is never shown again.

RegisteredPrincipal.title: RegisteredPrincipal
RegisteredPrincipal.description: Response to a successful registration. ``api_key`` is returned exactly once.

ReputationEventResponse.title: ReputationEventResponse
ReputationEventResponse.description: One entry in a provider's reputation ledger — why the score moved (Session 11.6).

ResourceSpec.title: ResourceSpec
ResourceSpec.description: Hardware a job needs. The scheduler matches these against provider capabilities.

SessionResponse.title: SessionResponse
SessionResponse.description:
An established wallet session. ``api_key`` is the session credential: it goes
straight into the httpOnly cookie and is never shown to the user.

UploadCreateRequest.title: UploadCreateRequest
UploadCreateRequest.description: Start a resumable upload, optionally declaring the final sha256 for verification.

UploadSessionResponse.title: UploadSessionResponse
UploadSessionResponse.description: An upload session's state, including bytes received (the resume offset).

VerifyRequest.title: VerifyRequest
VerifyRequest.description:
A signed challenge. ``nonce`` selects the stored message to check the signature
against; the message text itself is never accepted from the client.
